data.title: Crypto-tied stocks lead week's financial winners; Argentine banks tumble
data.description:
This week's biggest gainers across financials were led by crypto-linked stocks on the back of rising token prices, while Argentine lenders fell the most amid rising political uncertainty.

Cipher Mining (CIFR) rose the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B), surging 44% in the same week it delivered bitcoin (BTC-USD) production numbers for August;

BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), a crypto treasury firm, spiked 31% in the week ended Sept. 12, as its crypto holdings continued to swell;

IREN (IREN), which during the week posted an 8% monthly slide in August bitcoin (BTC-USD) production, accelerated 30%;

Fellow crypto miner Hut 8 (HUT) gained 28%; and

Crypto investment firm Galaxy Digital (GLXY) rounded out the five biggest financial winners, climbing 26%, after news it's allowing its stockholders to tokenize their class A common shares on the Solana (SOL-USD) network via blockchain firm Superstate's Opening Bell platform.

On the losers, Argentina-based Banco Macro (BMA) and Grupo Financiero Galicia (GGAL) shares dropped 26% and 25%, respectively, driven by Argentine President Javier Milei's ruling party losing in a key provincial election in the Buenos Aires province;

From there, Accelerant Holdings (ARX) shed 15%;

SLM (SLM) retreated 9.6%; and

Credit Acceptance (CACC) slid 9.2%.

data.title: U.S. semiconductor sector targeted in new China investigations
data.description:
The Ministry of Commerce in China announced on Saturday two investigations targeting the U.S. semiconductor sector, where tech giants such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) dominate.

According to a statement on the ministry’s website, the ministry launched an anti-dumping investigation on Sept. 13 targeting imports of certain analog chips manufactured in the U.S.

Another statement announced the launch of an investigation targeting what it identified as “anti-discrimination measures” against China’s integrated circuit sector.

Relevant tickers: Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), Broadcom (AVGO), Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC), Texas Instruments (TXN), Marvell Technology (MRVL), QUALCOMM (QCOM), Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), Analog Devices (ADI), Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX).

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: Employer health plans are set to face the biggest cost hike in 15 years
data.description:
Employer-sponsored healthcare plans in the U.S. are set to face the biggest cost hike in 15 years next year, leading to pay cuts and larger out-of-pocket expenses for employees amid rising healthcare prices and utilization, according to a recent survey from HR consulting firm Mercer.

Using responses obtained from more than 1,700 employers through Aug. 12, the survey shows that the total health benefit cost is expected to increase 6.5% on average per employee next year, marking the biggest rise since 2010.

While Mercer's projection assumes cost reduction measures from employers, the average total health benefit cost is expected to rise further to the 9% level if employers don’t revise their current plans.

2026 will mark the fourth consecutive year when health benefit costs have grown significantly following a decade of modest annual increases averaging about 3%, Mercer said last week, announcing preliminary results from its 2025 National Survey of Employer-Sponsored Health Plans.

“Health benefit cost trend has two primary components—healthcare price and utilization. Right now, both are rising,” said Sunit Patel, Mercer’s US Chief Actuary for Health and Benefits, as reasons for the surge.

While treatments such as cancer medicines and newer GLP-1 weight loss drugs from Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (LLY) lead to better health outcomes, they typically come with higher price tags than the treatments they replace. Among other factors leading to price rises are inflation and the consolidation of healthcare providers, which increases their ability to negotiate reimbursement levels.

The popularity of virtual care and pent-up demand for delayed or missed medical care after the pandemic, along with other factors, have led to strong utilization rates, which Patel said have been rising over the past two years.

“The rise of virtual healthcare—and growing consumer acceptance of it, particularly in behavioral health—is also affecting utilization patterns because it removes geographic barriers to care and can be a more convenient option for patients,” he added.

While 48% and 44% of employers implemented cost-cutting changes to their plans in 2025 and 2024, respectively, in 2026, 59% of employers are set to introduce cost-cutting measures such as higher deductibles, which can lead to higher out-of-pocket costs for employees.

Additionally, employees can also expect a 6% - 7% average increase in paycheck deductions for health coverage in 2026, as rising plan costs also cause a proportional increase in their share of premiums.

Health insurers: UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Cigna (NYSE:CI), Clover Health (CLOV), Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (NYSE:CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), Elevance Health (ELV)

data.title: BlackRock said to unveil £500M investment in U.K. data centers during Trump visit
data.description:
• BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) plans to announce a £500M ($678M) investment in U.K. data centers next week during U.S. President Donald Trump’s visit to the country, Sky News reported on Saturday.
  • The New York-based private equity firm also plans to announce a joint venture with Digital Gravity Partners, a digital infrastructure investment manager. The JV will focus on buying and modernizing existing data centers to improve their capacity.
  • Additionally, the world’s largest money manager is set to unveil a new office location in Edinburgh, which would employ about 1,300 people, adding to its already substantial business presence in Britain.
  • BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) CEO Larry Fink is also expected to be part of the business delegation accompanying Trump during his second state visit to the U.K., which is scheduled to take place from Sept. 17 to 19.

data.title: Loan document shows Fed's Cook listed Georgia residence as vacation home - report
data.description:
• A loan estimate provided by a credit union that Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook used for a mortgage on a residence she purchased in Atlanta shows she claimed it as a "vacation home."
  • The document, dated May 28, 2021, is significant in that it shows she told the credit union the Georgia home would not be her primary residence, Reuters reported. President Trump has accused Cook of committing mortgage fraud by claiming more than one property as her primary residence in an effort to oust her from the Fed.
  • The development comes at a crucial time for the central bank as it holds a monetary policy meeting next week that Cook will take part in.
  • Another document Reuters reviewed, which was used by Cook as part of her Fed security clearance, indicated that in December 2021, she declared the Atlanta house her "2nd home."
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.description:

  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of common stock by the selling shareholder.

data.title: ECD Automotive Design plunges after announcing 1-for-40 reverse stock split
data.description:
• ECD Automotive Design(NASDAQ:ECDA) will implement a 1-for-40 reverse stock split of its issued and outstanding common stock, effective at market opening time on September 18, 2025.
  • The split will reduce the number of outstanding shares of ECD’s common stock from about 59.09M shares to about 1.47M shares.
  • ECDA -30.95% after hours to $0.0946.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Telesat announces equity distribution of Telesat Lightspeed business
data.description:
• Telesat (NASDAQ:TSAT) said on Friday that it has completed the distribution of 62% of the equity of its Telesat Lightspeed business to an indirect subsidiary of Telesat Corporation. 
  • The indirect subsidiary is wholly-owned by Telesat Canada’s parent entities and is a non-guarantor under Telesat Canada’s debt documents. 
  • There are no changes to the company’s operations as a result of this transaction.
